_comment: Instance-ID shape contract for Patina <-> Godot 4.6.1 structural compatibility.
_doc: Patina's NodeId/ObjectId system must satisfy these shape invariants when compared against Godot's get_instance_id(). Value equality is NOT required — only structural shape.

contract.uniqueness: globally unique per session — no two live nodes share an ID
contract.stability: immutable for the lifetime of the node — survives reparenting, renaming, property mutation
contract.monotonic: IDs are allocated in strictly increasing order (creation order)
contract.recycling: IDs of removed nodes are never reused
contract.display_format: plain decimal integer string (no prefix, no hex)
contract.variant_type: Int (i64 in Patina Variant, maps to Godot Variant.Type.INT)

oracle_property_mapping._comment: Instance IDs are NOT serialised as properties in oracle fixtures — they are runtime-only method returns (Object.get_instance_id()). When oracle property fixtures include process_thread_group in the future, it will appear as an int property with values: INHERIT=0, MAIN_THREAD=1, SUB_THREAD=2.
